description_long: This is a two-story wooden house with a steep, dark-shingled gable roof and a prominent central dormer. The exterior features horizontal wooden siding with a weathered, yellowish-brown finish, and the roof has visible wear, including a broken section. The front facade includes a covered porch with a small overhang and multiple rectangular windows, some of which are missing panes or boarded up. The side of the house has an external staircase leading to a secondary entrance on the upper floor, and a large brick chimney rises from the rear. The house appears aged and slightly dilapidated, with structural supports and railings that show signs of disrepair. Overall, the design suggests a late 19th to early 20th-century American vernacular style, possibly influenced by Victorian or Folk architecture.
description: A weathered, two-story wooden house with a steep gable roof, central dormer, covered porch, and external staircase. The structure shows signs of age and disrepair, with boarded windows and a prominent brick chimney.
description_view_0: Front view showing the covered porch, central dormer, and main entryway with multiple windows.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the external staircase, secondary upper entrance, and large brick chimney.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back porch, boarded windows, and the broken section of the roof.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with visible wooden siding, secondary entrance, and structural supports.
attribute_entryway_design: Main entryway features a covered porch with steps, simple wooden columns, and a small overhang.
